title: Walking in Truth and Love
subtitle: The elder to the elect lady, warning against deceivers, avoiding false teachers

heb.paragraph: The elder to the elect lady and her children - either a specific woman or a personified church. Elect emphasizes divine choosing.
Whom I love in the truth - truth is both the sphere and standard of love. The elder loves because of and in accordance with truth.
Walking in truth and love - the same dual emphasis as 1 John. Truth and love are inseparable marks of genuine faith.

panels.ctx: The elder to the elect lady and her children, whom I love in the truth, and not only I but also all who know the truth, because of the truth that abides in us and will be with us forever. Grace, mercy, and peace will be with us from God the Father and from Jesus Christ the Father Son, in truth and love. I rejoiced greatly to find some of your children walking in the truth, just as we were commanded by the Father. And now I ask you, dear lady - not as though I were writing you a new commandment, but the one we have had from the beginning - that we love one another. And this is love, that we walk according to his commandments; this is the commandment, just as you have heard from the beginning, so that you should walk in it.

cross.note: I am writing you no new commandment but an old commandment.
A new commandment I give to you, that you love one another.
I have no greater joy than to hear that my children are walking in the truth.
This is the love of God, that we keep his commandments.

notes.note: The elder to the elect lady and her children, whom I love in truth. Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Jesus Christ his Son.
I rejoiced that some of your children walk in truth. Love one another - the commandment from the beginning. This is love: walk according to his commandments.
Calvin notes that the elder title suggests apostolic authority without claiming the apostle designation explicitly.
Walking in truth and walking in love are the same thing expressed differently. Truth-walking is love-walking.

netbible.source: NET Bible Full Notes Edition — Biblical Studies Press

notes.note: The elect lady (eklekte kyria) could be a specific woman named Kyria, a woman described as elect, or a personified church.
Not a new commandment but the one we have had from the beginning - echoes 1 John 2:7. The commandment is old yet ever-renewed.

yarbrough.source: Robert Yarbrough, BECNT 1-3 John — Scholarly Paraphrase

notes.note: Yarbrough emphasizes the interplay of truth and love throughout. Neither exists without the other in authentic Christianity.

kruse.source: Colin Kruse, TNTC/Pillar 1-3 John — Scholarly Paraphrase

notes.note: Kruse notes that walking in truth involves both doctrinal fidelity and ethical obedience. Truth is lived, not just believed.

heb.paragraph: Many deceivers have gone out into the world - false teachers are active and widespread. Vigilance is required.

heb.gloss: to go before, go ahead, advance beyond
heb.paragraph: Everyone who goes on ahead and does not abide in the teaching of Christ - progressive departure from apostolic doctrine.
Do not give him a greeting - the greeting implied endorsement and partnership. Withholding it maintained doctrinal boundaries.

panels.ctx: For many deceivers have gone out into the world, those who do not confess the coming of Jesus Christ in the flesh. Such a one is the deceiver and the antichrist. Watch yourselves, so that you may not lose what we have worked for, but may win a full reward. Everyone who goes on ahead and does not abide in the teaching of Christ does not have God. Whoever abides in the teaching has both the Father and the Son. If anyone comes to you and does not bring this teaching, do not receive him into your house or give him any greeting, for whoever greets him takes part in his wicked works.

cross.note: Test the spirits; many false prophets have gone out.
Those who do not confess Jesus Christ coming in the flesh.
If anyone will not receive you, shake off the dust.
As for a person who stirs up division, warn him, then avoid him.

notes.note: Many deceivers have gone out, not confessing Jesus Christ coming in flesh. This is the deceiver and antichrist. Watch yourselves; win full reward.
Whoever goes ahead and does not abide in Christ teaching does not have God. Do not receive him into your house or greet him - or you share in his wicked works.
Calvin emphasizes the christological test: denial of incarnation is antichrist. Progress beyond apostolic teaching is regression.
The restriction on hospitality is severe but necessary. False teachers must not be given platform or endorsement.

netbible.source: NET Bible Full Notes Edition — Biblical Studies Press

notes.note: Coming of Jesus Christ in the flesh (erchomenon en sarki) - the present participle may emphasize ongoing incarnation, not just past event.
House (oikia) refers to the house church. Receiving a false teacher meant giving him a teaching platform in the congregation.

yarbrough.source: Robert Yarbrough, BECNT 1-3 John — Scholarly Paraphrase

notes.note: Yarbrough notes the tension between love and discernment. Love does not require welcoming those who deny the gospel.

kruse.source: Colin Kruse, TNTC/Pillar 1-3 John — Scholarly Paraphrase

notes.note: Kruse emphasizes that doctrinal boundaries protect the community. Hospitality to false teachers endangers the church.

heb.paragraph: Though I have much to write, I would rather not use paper and ink - personal presence is preferred over written communication.
That our joy may be complete - face-to-face fellowship brings fullness that letters cannot achieve.

panels.ctx: Though I have much to write to you, I would rather not use paper and ink. Instead I hope to come to you and talk face to face, so that our joy may be complete. The children of your elect sister greet you.

cross.note: I have much to write but hope to see you soon and talk face to face.
We write these things so that our joy may be complete.
That I may come to you with joy and be refreshed.

notes.note: I have much to write but prefer face-to-face conversation, so our joy may be complete. The children of your elect sister greet you.
Calvin notes that the elder prefers personal presence. Written communication has limits that face-to-face fellowship transcends.

netbible.source: NET Bible Full Notes Edition — Biblical Studies Press

notes.note: Your elect sister - if the elect lady is a church, the elect sister is a sister church. Both are chosen by God.

yarbrough.source: Robert Yarbrough, BECNT 1-3 John — Scholarly Paraphrase

notes.note: Yarbrough notes the warmth of the closing. Despite the stern warning, the elder loves the community and anticipates joyful reunion.

kruse.source: Colin Kruse, TNTC/Pillar 1-3 John — Scholarly Paraphrase

notes.note: Kruse emphasizes that the brief letter is intentional - more will be said in person. The letter addresses urgent concerns.

themes.note: Faith is tested by christological confession. Holiness requires separation from false teachers. Justice appears in the warning about sharing in wicked works.

lit.note: 2 John is a brief letter combining exhortation to love with warning against deceivers. Truth and love must both be maintained.
